compiled_state.title: [sprint/brainstorm]: intent captured for env verification not translated into implementation -> sign-in non-functional at manual testing
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** Implementation gap — brainstorm intent not enforced in implementation plan

#### Observed Behavior

Sprint generated an implementation plan that used a P3 script testing only the AWS CLI auth path (admin-initiate-auth smoke test). This path bypasses Auth.js entirely, so it could not detect a broken browser OAuth callback. The verification gate reported 'admin-initiate-auth smoke passed' but the browser OAuth flow was broken. When the user attempted manual testing, the sign-in process was non-functional.

**Log from host session:**
`verification gate: admin-initiate-auth smoke passed → browser OAuth flow broken. The P3 script only tested the AWS CLI path (bypasses Auth.js entirely), so it couldn't catch the broken browser callback. The escalation gate wasn't actually enforced.`

#### Expected Behavior

The brainstorm session captured intent that the sprint should: (1) test the external environment it configured, and (2) verify the success of the sign-in process before escalating to the user for manual testing. The implementation plan should have enforced an end-to-end browser OAuth verification gate, not just an AWS CLI smoke test.

#### Root Cause (Hypothesis)

The sprint/implementation-plan skill did not translate the brainstorm-captured verification intent into an implementation plan that actually tested the Auth.js browser flow. The P3 verification step was scoped too narrowly (CLI-only), missing the browser callback path that is the actual user-facing sign-in mechanism.
